incidents.title: Database connection timeout on prod-db01
incidents.description: Production database server prod-db01 at 10.0.1.5 is not accepting connections. TCP port 5432 unreachable. Multiple application services reporting connection refused. Likely full outage affecting all backend services.

incidents.title: Suspicious login attempts from unknown IP
incidents.description: Over 200 failed SSH login attempts detected from IP 185.220.101.47 in the past 30 minutes. This looks like a brute-force attack targeting the admin account on web-server-02. Source IP not in allowlist.

incidents.title: Checkout service returning NullPointerException
incidents.description: The checkout microservice is throwing a NullPointerException on every POST /api/checkout request. Stack trace shows the error in CartService.java:142. HTTP 500 responses being returned to all users. App is failing.

incidents.title: Network switch offline in DC rack B
incidents.description: Core network switch SW-RACK-B-01 is down and unreachable on 192.168.10.2. All servers in rack B have lost connectivity. The switch handles VLAN 20 and VLAN 30 traffic. Production services in that rack are fully offline.

incidents.title: Ransomware signature detected on workstation
incidents.description: Endpoint detection tool flagged ransomware signatures on workstation WKSTN-042 assigned to finance team. Files in C:/Users/jsmith/Documents are being encrypted. The machine should be isolated immediately to prevent spread.

incidents.title: Payment gateway timeout — Stripe API 503
incidents.description: Payment service is receiving HTTP 503 errors from the Stripe API endpoint https://api.stripe.com/v1/charges. Requests are timing out after 30s. Error code STRIPE_TIMEOUT returned. All payment transactions failing.

incidents.title: Intermittent packet loss on VLAN 30
incidents.description: Network monitoring shows 15% intermittent packet loss on VLAN 30 between floors 3 and 4. TCP retransmissions increasing. Affecting users in the finance department. The issue appears to be on the uplink between SW-F3 at 192.168.30.1 and SW-F4.

incidents.title: Phishing email reported by 3 employees
incidents.description: Three employees in the marketing department have reported receiving a suspicious phishing email impersonating the IT department. The email contains a link to a credential harvesting page. Subject line: 'Urgent: Reset your password now'. Sender domain spoofed.

incidents.title: API response slow — P95 latency 4.2s
incidents.description: The product catalog API /api/products is experiencing degraded performance. P95 latency has risen to 4.2 seconds, up from a baseline of 200ms. The service is intermittently slow but not fully down. Likely a database query or cache issue.

incidents.title: Scheduled job failed — non-production env
incidents.description: The nightly ETL job on the staging environment failed at step 3 of 8. Error: FileNotFoundError — input CSV file missing. This is a non-production issue. The job is scheduled to run again tomorrow. No user impact. Low priority.

incidents.title: Minor firewall rule misconfiguration found
incidents.description: A security audit found a minor misconfiguration in firewall rule FW-RULE-204 allowing outbound traffic on port 8080 from the DMZ. This is a low-risk finding. No active exploitation detected. Should be corrected in the next change window.

incidents.title: DNS resolution intermittent on 10.2.0.0/16
incidents.description: Several hosts on the 10.2.0.0/16 subnet are reporting intermittent DNS resolution failures. nslookup times out approximately 30% of the time. The primary DNS server at 10.2.0.1 appears to be responding slowly. Issue is intermittent and affecting a subset of users.
